## Speculation vs. Fact
<!-- ⚠️ CRITICAL: Clearly distinguish established science from speculation -->

**This PR contains**:
- [ ] Only established, proven technology (TRL 7-9)
- [ ] Mixture of proven and speculative elements
- [ ] Primarily speculative/theoretical content (TRL 1-3)

**Speculative Claims Labeling**:
- [ ] All speculative content tagged with `(SPECULATIVE)`
- [ ] All hypothetical scenarios tagged with `(HYPOTHETICAL)`
- [ ] All unproven technology tagged with `(UNPROVEN)`
- [ ] TRL levels clearly indicated

**Example Labeling**:
```markdown
The constellation uses Ka-band frequencies (26.5-40 GHz) [ESTABLISHED - TRL 9].

**(SPECULATIVE - TRL 1)**: Neuromorphic processors on satellites could
process consciousness data in orbit, though radiation hardening for
neural networks is unproven.
```

## Technical Accuracy Review
<!-- Self-review checklist for technical accuracy -->

### Physics/Engineering
- [ ] Latency calculations verified (LEO = 5-15ms one-way)
- [ ] Bandwidth estimates justified (2-20 Gbps corpus callosum)
- [ ] Power budgets realistic
- [ ] Orbital mechanics correct
- [ ] Link budgets calculated

### Neuroscience
- [ ] Neural data rates scientifically grounded
- [ ] Consciousness claims cite peer-reviewed sources
- [ ] No pseudoscience or unsupported claims
- [ ] Clear distinction between correlation and causation

### Systems Engineering
- [ ] Architecture diagrams accurate
- [ ] Integration points well-defined
- [ ] Failure modes considered
- [ ] Redundancy documented

## Documentation Quality
- [ ] Clear and concise writing
- [ ] Technical terminology defined
- [ ] Acronyms defined on first use
- [ ] Diagrams included (if applicable)
- [ ] Cross-references to other documents correct
- [ ] Formatting consistent (Markdown, YAML syntax)
- [ ] No spelling or grammar errors
- [ ] README updated (if applicable)
